query: Draft a one-page daily task list template for a retail electronics department supervisor. Include space for employee names, initials, manager sign-off, and end-of-day filing instructions.
Write a professional email to a vendor requesting a two-week extension on a delayed shipment, citing inventory impact without sounding accusatory.
Create a brief SOP for onboarding a new cashier: opening shift checklist, cash handling, and escalation to manager.
Outline a meeting agenda for a weekly team standup in a small software support team (15 minutes max, 5 agenda items).
Produce a customer-facing FAQ entry explaining a 14-day return policy for opened electronics, including exceptions for defective items.
Write a shift handoff note template for a hospital ward nurse covering patient status, pending labs, and follow-ups.
Draft a short incident report template for a warehouse supervisor: incident date/time, location, people involved, immediate actions, and follow-up owner.

Write an internal memo email to your director requesting approval for a $12,000 equipment purchase. Explain business impact, alternatives considered, and a proposed timeline. Keep it professional and under one page.

Customer cannot send MMS. Data works for browsing. List diagnostic steps including APN, MMS gateway settings, and Wi-Fi calling interference.
User wants to port number from another carrier. Account PIN verified. What steps must complete before port initiation and what failures require human agent?
Roaming enabled but no data abroad. Passport plan active. Device shows LTE but no internet. Troubleshooting sequence?
Dual-SIM phone: calls work on SIM1 but not SIM2. Both lines active. What should agent verify?

Based on the policy excerpt, how many days does an employee have to submit expense reports after travel end date?
contextText: Travel Policy Section 4.2: All receipts and expense reports must be submitted within 10 business days of return. Late submissions require director approval. Per diem rates follow GSA tables for the travel destination.

query: From the contract clause below, who bears shipping cost for returns within the warranty period?
contextText: Section 8.1 Warranty Returns: Seller shall provide prepaid return label for defective units within 90 days of delivery. Buyer pays freight for returns due to buyer-caused damage. Refunds processed within 14 days of receipt at warehouse.

query: According to the meeting minutes, which team owns the API rate limit change and what is the target date?
contextText: Minutes 2026-03-15: Platform team (lead: Priya) will implement Redis-backed rate limiting by 2026-04-01. Mobile team to update client backoff by 2026-03-28. Security review scheduled 2026-03-22.

query: What maximum batch size does the ingestion pipeline allow per the design doc excerpt?
contextText: Ingestion v2 design: Batches up to 5000 records per POST. Payload max 8MB. Exceeding limits returns 413. Retry with exponential backoff on 429. Dead letter queue after 5 failures.

query: Which two databases are mentioned as read replicas in the architecture note?
contextText: Production Postgres primary in us-east-1. Read replicas: analytics-replica-1 (us-east-1), reporting-replica-2 (eu-west-1). Connection pooler: PgBouncer 1.22. Failover via Patroni.

Brainstorm five distinct go-to-market angles for a multi-agent AI council product aimed at senior engineers. For each angle, note target user, wedge, and one measurable success metric.
I need creative but realistic pricing models for a B2B devtool with heavy LLM usage. Compare usage-based, seat-based, and hybrid approaches with pros and cons.
Review this launch claim for an AI council chat app: 'We run five Mercury calls on every message so answers are always correct.' Identify factual, marketing, and trust risks.
Critique storing conversation history only in browser localStorage for a localhost-first prototype that may later add cloud sync. Cover privacy, durability, and migration.

Compare managed Postgres (Neon), Supabase Postgres, and self-hosted Postgres on a single Hetzner box for a Next.js SaaS with moderate writes, preview branches, EU data residency, and a team of four engineers. We care about operational burden, branching workflows, connection pooling, auth integration, cost at ~500GB storage and 50M rows, and escape hatches if we outgrow the vendor. Which would you pick for year one and what would trigger a migration?

Compare running a full three-member council on every user message versus tiered routing (single for precision tasks, lite for procedural/debug, full only for brainstorm/review/long deliverables) for a Mercury-based product. Discuss quality on AA-shaped evals, latency, token cost, failure modes, and what to measure in regression before shipping tiered routing broadly.

Brainstorm three viable architecture patterns for a multi-tenant B2B SaaS that must handle 10k concurrent websocket connections, strict tenant isolation, and hourly batch exports. For each pattern, argue the strongest case for and against it, then name the failure mode that would make you abandon it.

Review this API design for a file-upload service:

POST /upload?token={jwt}
Body: multipart file, no size cap
Response: public URL with predictable UUID
No rate limits, admin key in README

List concrete security and abuse risks ranked by severity.
Compare Redis versus Memcached for a session cache on a single-region Next.js app with 50k DAU, 24h TTL, and occasional thundering-herd on login. Pick one for year one.
Given our earlier discussion, which routing mode would you ship first and what would you measure before enabling full council on compare?

priorTurns.query: We're building tiered council routing: single for MCQ, lite for debug, full for brainstorm. Compare quality vs cost tradeoffs.
priorTurns.synthesisMarkdown: Single-shot wins on latency and cost for objective tasks. Lite council covers medium debug with one member plus chair. Full council pays off when synthesis across disagreeing perspectives matters—brainstorm, security review, long compares.
